behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is the CLI reading an actual git repo state and interpreting real rename status from git outputs/index state. A unit test of categorization logic can miss failures caused by subprocess output shape, rename detection metadata, or parser handoff. Considered Integration, but rejected because behavior text is about actual repository rename outcomes, not just isolated in-memory mapping.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo, commit `a.txt`, rename to `b.txt` (`git mv` + commit or working-tree rename as supported), run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert report shows `renamed=1` with `added=0` and `deleted=0` for that change set.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real binary diff handling through git and CLI I/O boundaries; mocks often hide decode/encoding and subprocess-output edge cases. No LLM dependency. Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text explicitly targets real binary diffs causing crashes, which usually appear at real git/process boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, add/modify binary files (e.g., random bytes), run CLI subprocess over those changes, assert zero crash (exit code 0) and binary changes are included in category counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Empty-history repos trigger real git/HEAD edge behavior (`unborn` branch, log/diff errors) at process/repository boundary. A mocked repo client can miss this exact failure mode. Considered Integration, but rejected because behavior text refers to an actual repo state with no commits, which is a real git environment edge.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run CLI subprocess, assert successful exit and valid empty structured report (no categories counted, no error output).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: The behavior validates end-to-end correctness against real repository history, requiring real git history traversal plus CLI/report wiring. Unit tests can validate counting logic but not failures from history extraction/parsing boundaries. Considered Integration, but rejected because behavior text requires matching the actual repository history, implying real repo/process interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a known sequence of commits containing adds/deletes/renames/modifications, run CLI subprocess for the target range, and assert report counts exactly match the known ground truth from created history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics are git index/worktree behaviors at real repository boundary; mocks can hide differences in how git reports each state. No AI dependency. Considered Integration, but rejected because behavior text explicitly compares real staged/unstaged repository states.
behaviors.test_description: Create equivalent change sets in two temp repos (or reset between runs): one staged, one unstaged. Run CLI subprocess in both states and assert identical structured stdout output.
